summary: After unpausing, Minecraft keeps fluctuating between 5 and 30 FPS and doesn t return to normal.
description: I left the game paused for a while, and when I came back, the FPS was much lower and wouldn t return to normal. This isn t the first time it has happened in this snapshot. n n
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Launch Minecraft and select the latest snapshot version from the "Installations" tab.
2. Create a new single-player world with default settings.
3. Enter the world and play for a few minutes to establish a normal frame rate (above 30 FPS).
4. Press the Esc key to open the pause menu and select "Resume Game" to pause the game.
5. Leave the game paused for at least 10 minutes without interacting with it.
6. After the pause duration, press the Esc key again to unpause the game.
7. Observe the frame rate displayed in the debug screen by pressing F3 and note the fluctuations between 5 and 30 FPS.
8. Continue to play the game for several minutes to confirm that the frame rate does not return to normal levels.
Observed Behavior:
Upon unpausing the game after an extended period of inactivity, the frame rate drops significantly, fluctuating between 5 and 30 FPS. This performance issue persists and does not stabilize back to normal levels, which typically range from 60 FPS or higher under standard gameplay conditions. The FPS drop occurs regardless of the world settings or the number of entities present in the game. 

This issue has been observed multiple times in the same snapshot and seems to occur consistently after the game has been paused for a prolonged duration (generally more than 10 minutes). During this period, the game appears to maintain its resource allocation, but upon resuming, it struggles to render frames efficiently, leading to a choppy and unplayable experience. 

Players can verify the FPS drop by accessing the debug screen using the F3 key, which displays the current frame rate alongside other performance metrics. This fluctuation does not seem to correlate with any specific in-game events, such as chunk loading or high entity counts, as it occurs independently of these factors. 

Additionally, restarting the game or reloading the world does not resolve the issue, indicating that it may be related to the game's handling of resources after being paused. This behavior has been reported by multiple users, suggesting it may be a broader issue affecting players in this snapshot.
Expected Behavior: When a player unpauses Minecraft after being in the pause menu, the game should resume at a stable frame rate, ideally returning to the previously experienced FPS levels before the pause. The game should not exhibit any significant fluctuations in frame rate, and performance should remain consistent with the expected behavior of the game. If the player's system is capable of running Minecraft at a higher FPS, it should maintain that performance without dropping to lower levels, ensuring a smooth and seamless gaming experience. The game should also properly manage resources and not suffer from performance degradation after being paused for an extended period, as outlined in the official guidelines for optimizing frame rates in Minecraft.

comments.body_text:
*We do not have enough information to find the cause of this issue.*

Please **record a video of this happening** and attach it to this report.If you are on Windows, you can use `Windows`+`Alt`+`R` to open a built-in app for recording game footage.If you are on Mac (Mojave or later), you can use `Shift`+`Command`+`5` to open a built-in app for recording your screen.In case you don't have a program to record videos, we recommend using the free recording software OBS.In case the resulting video file is too large to be uploaded to the bug tracker directly, please upload it elsewhere (e.g. as unlisted video on YouTube) and link to it here.

This issue is being temporarily resolved as **Awaiting Response**. Once the requested information has been delivered, the report will be reopened automatically.*Quick Links*:📓 Bug Tracker Guidelines -- 💬 Community Support -- 📧 Mojang Support (Technical Issues) -- 📧 Microsoft Support (Account Issues) -- 📓 Project Summary -- ✍️ Feedback and Suggestions -- 📖 Game Wiki

Please record a video of this happening and attach it to this report.
If you are on Windows, you can use WindowsAltR to open a built-in app for recording game footage.
If you are on Mac (Mojave or later), you can use ShiftCommand5 to open a built-in app for recording your screen.
In case you don't have a program to record videos, we recommend using the free recording software OBS.
In case the resulting video file is too large to be uploaded to the bug tracker directly, please upload it elsewhere (e.g. as unlisted video on YouTube) and link to it here.

This issue is being temporarily resolved as Awaiting Response. Once the requested information has been delivered, the report will be reopened automatically.
